title: spawn_opt/2,4 — spawn with options
purpose: `erlang:spawn_opt/2` and `erlang:spawn_opt/4` spawn a process with a list of options: `link`, `monitor`, `{priority, Level}`, `{min_heap_size, Words}`, `{fullsweep_after, N}`. OTP supervisors use spawn_opt to configure child processes at spawn time. Without it, supervisor modules that pass spawn options fail.
task: Implement spawn_opt/2 (fun + options) and spawn_opt/4 (MFA + options) as BIFs that parse the option list and configure the spawned process accordingly.

requirements.title: Option list parsing
requirements.spec: Parse the spawn options list into a `SpawnOptions` struct.
acceptance: `SpawnOptions` struct with fields: `link: bool`, `monitor: bool`, `priority: Option<Priority>`, `min_heap_size: Option<usize>`
Parse function extracts known options from a proper list, ignoring unknown atoms (BEAM silently ignores unknown options)
Test: parse `[link, monitor, {priority, high}]` → correct SpawnOptions

requirements.title: erlang:spawn_opt/4
requirements.spec: Register `erlang:spawn_opt/4` accepting (Module, Function, Args, Options). Returns Pid or {Pid, Ref} depending on monitor option.
acceptance: Without monitor option: returns Pid (same as spawn/3)
With monitor option: returns {Pid, Ref} (same as spawn_monitor/3)
With link option: link established atomically (same as spawn_link/3)
With priority option: child process starts with specified priority
With min_heap_size option: child process heap starts at specified size
Test: spawn_opt with [link, {priority, high}] → child is linked and high-priority

requirements.title: erlang:spawn_opt/2
requirements.spec: Register `erlang:spawn_opt/2` accepting (Fun, Options). Same semantics as spawn_opt/4 but with a fun.
acceptance: Accepts a fun term + options list
Return type depends on monitor option (Pid or {Pid, Ref})
Test: spawn_opt with a closure and [monitor] → returns {Pid, Ref}

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement all spawn_opt options — only: link, monitor, {priority, Level}, {min_heap_size, Words}
SHALL NOT implement {fullsweep_after, N} — GC tuning is out of scope for now
SHALL NOT implement {message_queue_data, off_heap} — message queue optimization is out of scope
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
